cold.label: Cold (60–80°F)
cold.when: Dark colors, bright dyes, lightly soiled items

hot.label: Hot (120°F+)
hot.why: Best cleaning but HIGH shrink/fade risk

cold.label: Cold (60–80°F)
cold.when: All wool items — especially hand-knit or loosely woven pieces
cold.why: Cold water prevents felting and fiber shrinkage

warm.label: Warm (90–110°F)
warm.when: Almost never — only if label explicitly says warm is safe
warm.why: Warm water begins to felt wool fibers together permanently

hot.label: Hot (120°F+)
hot.when: Never — hot water will ruin wool irreversibly
hot.why: Causes severe felting, shrinkage, and fiber damage

cold.label: Cold (60–80°F)
cold.why: Prevents heat warping, fiber damage, and static buildup

warm.label: Warm (90–110°F)
warm.when: Moderately soiled polyester shirts or pants
warm.why: OK for sturdy synthetics; check the label first

hot.label: Hot (120°F+)
hot.when: Almost never — only if label explicitly allows
hot.why: High heat melts synthetic fibers, causes permanent damage

cold.label: Hand Wash
cold.when: Silk, lace, lingerie, any item labeled 'hand wash'

warm.label: Delicate Cycle (Cold)
warm.when: Sturdy delicates in a mesh laundry bag
warm.why: Low agitation + cold water = safe for most delicates

hot.label: Warm / Hot Water
hot.why: Heat shrinks silk, distorts lace, ruins elastics

settings.best_for: Best for all cotton — no risk
Never recommended — low heat still risks felting
Never — causes irreversible felting and shrink
Always — lay flat on a towel to preserve shape
Silk, lace, any stretchy delicate. Preserves shape.
Silk blouses. Avoid for heavy items — stretches them.
Only if label explicitly allows. Use a mesh bag. Remove immediately.
NEVER — destroys lace, elastic, and silk permanently.

tips: Remove while slightly damp to reduce wrinkles
Whites can yellow in high heat over time
Always use a wool-safe or delicate detergent — no regular detergent
Never wring or twist wet wool — press gently in a towel
Lay flat to dry to preserve the original shape
Store folded, never hung — hanging stretches the shoulders
Hand wash is always safer than any machine cycle
Wash activewear inside-out to protect wicking
Add a dryer sheet to fight static cling
Nylon & spandex: always air dry if possible
Pilling? Use a mesh laundry bag next time
Always use a mesh laundry bag when machine washing
Never wring or twist — press gently in a clean towel to remove water
Use a detergent formulated for delicates or silk
Air dry flat on a towel — never hang stretchy delicates
Silk blouses can be hung on a padded hanger to dry

common_mistakes.do: Fill ¾ full for best clean
Check the label — use medium or low
